title: PCOS — implied, contradicted, not-supported
notes: Synthetic; controlled ground truth. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: More women ovulated with the treatment than with placebo.1 The treatment improved live birth rates.1 The treatment improved long-term metabolic outcomes.2

goldClaims.originalText: More women ovulated with the treatment than with placebo.1
goldClaims.text: More women ovulated with the treatment than with placebo

goldClaims.originalText: The treatment improved live birth rates.1
goldClaims.text: The treatment improved live birth rates

goldClaims.originalText: The treatment improved long-term metabolic outcomes.2
goldClaims.text: The treatment improved long-term metabolic outcomes

1.name: PCOS-OV (Ali 2023)
1.text: Table 3. Ovulation rate: treatment 62%, placebo 28%. Live birth rates were similar between the treatment and placebo groups (24% vs 22%; not significant).

2.name: PCOS-OV follow-up
2.text: Long-term metabolic outcomes such as incident diabetes were not evaluated during the study.

goldVerdicts.claimText: More women ovulated with the treatment than with placebo
goldVerdicts.rationale: Support is a data table (ovulation 62% vs 28%) with no narrative sentence (rule 5).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The treatment improved live birth rates
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref says live birth rates were similar (24% vs 22%, not significant) — negates an improvement (rule 1).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The treatment improved long-term metabolic outcomes
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref says metabolic outcomes were not evaluated — silent (rule 2).
